name: Nelson-Barr mechanism
summary: An alternative resolution of the strong-CP problem: CP is exact at tree level (so theta = 0 by symmetry), and CP is then spontaneously broken in a way that produces the CKM phase without re-introducing a tree-level theta. Heavy vector-like quarks mix with SM quarks through a CP-violating singlet sector; the resulting effective theta_eff is naturally small.

addresses_puzzles.note: Avoids the axion; the cost is heavy vector-like quarks and tuned scalar-sector parameters to keep the radiative theta from regenerating above the experimental bound.

touches_benchmarks.note: Generic Nelson-Barr models predict EDMs at or just below current bounds; viable parameter space is being closed by ongoing experiments.

new_symmetries: CP (exact at tree level, spontaneously broken)
introduces.note: Vector-like quark masses, mixing matrix, scalar singlet potential, and CP-violating phase together introduce ~5 new free parameters.

history: Nelson (1984) and Barr (1984) independently. Revived periodically as the axion window narrows. Modern variants address radiative stability of theta = 0 below the percent level.

references.citation: Nelson, 'Naturally weak CP violation', Phys. Lett. B 136, 387 (1984)
Barr, 'Solving the strong CP problem without the Peccei-Quinn symmetry', PRL 53, 329 (1984)
